# Tableflow 5-minute Overview Video

**Owner**: [Kyle Klein](mailto:kklein@confluent.io)  
**Stakeholders**: [Yashwanth Dasari](mailto:ydasari@confluent.io), [Ahmed Saef Zamzam](mailto:azamzam@confluent.io), [Kasun Indrasiri Gamage](mailto:kindrasiri@confluent.io), [Chris Holly](mailto:cholly@confluent.io), [Martin Lee](mailto:clee@confluent.io) (Eng), [Jeremy Ber](mailto:jber@confluent.io)

# Summary

The purpose of this document is to align all key Tableflow PM \+ PMM \+ Eng stakeholders on what is said (content) and what is shown (demo) about Tableflow in a \~5-minute overview video.

The ultimate goal is to output an evergreen asset that is refreshed every 4-6 months and is in length, format and style to the  [\~5-minute CP Overview](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1FjC8_Oy2kA8Mipwdz718ir15PX53XYw0/view?usp=drive_link)

# Intended Use

This video is intended to be used in these ways:

1. Played at demo booths at various events, including Current.  
2. Shown or referenced as a resource for internal enablement via webinars or static link  
3. Published on Confluent’s YouTube account

# Resources

[Kyle Klein](mailto:kklein@confluent.io) is using these resources as sources for content, both text and slides/images/videos, in the storyboard

## Content Scope

* [Meeting summary from Dec 3rd, 2025](https://confluent.slack.com/archives/C0A0N1ZMSGY/p1764862979386269)

# Storyboard

**Stakeholder Reviews**  
TMM: 	[Ahmed Saef Zamzam](mailto:azamzam@confluent.io) ✅  
PMM:  	[Yashwanth Dasari](mailto:ydasari@confluent.io) ✅  
PM: 	[Kasun Indrasiri Gamage](mailto:kindrasiri@confluent.io) ✅  
[Chris Holly](mailto:cholly@confluent.io) ✅(see comments)  
[Martin Lee](mailto:clee@confluent.io) ✅  
SE:	[Jeremy Ber](mailto:jber@confluent.io)✅(see comments)

| Part 1 Intro (0.75 mins) |  |  |
| :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **Timing** | **Visual** | **Narration** |
| 40 seconds | ![][image1] ![][image2]  ![][image3]  | **Overview** If you’re streaming business data with Kafka and struggling with brittle ETL jobs just to get that data into your lake or warehouse, then this video is for you\! In just a few minutes, you’ll see how **Confluent Tableflow** turns Kafka topics into analytics‑ready tables, without the usual pipeline pain. ~~Together we’ll learn: How Tableflow materializes topics as open-standard tables with catalog integration How it improves data quality and performance with a shift‑left approach How it handles security and governance for critical production workloads Let’s start with \#1~~ |
| **Part 2 Open Standards and Catalog Integrations (1.5 mins)** |  |  |
| **Timing** | **Visual** | **Narration** |
| 20 seconds | ![][image4] | At its core, **Tableflow materializes Kafka topics or Flink tables as Apache Iceberg or Delta Lake tables in just a few clicks** \- **all without duplicating data**\! That means any stream in Confluent Cloud can show up as a queryable table in your lakehouse or warehouse, ~~without you wiring up custom consumers, conversion jobs, or one‑off Glue scripts.~~   |
|  | Show a Postgres connector, then the CDC topic, then the topic in flink | The Confluent Data Streaming Platform understands Change Data Capture (CDC) and Debezium, which enables Tableflow to efficiently materialize change logs as tables. This approach keeps analytic views of operational databases fresh, without hand‑rolled upsert logic. |
| 25 seconds | Going back to the topic, Start to enable Tableflow on it and zoom in on storage options | You’re not locked into a single storage pattern either. With **flexible storage options**, you can: Use **Confluent Managed Storage**, where Confluent handles the underlying object storage for you, or Choose **Bring Your Own Storage (BYOS)** and land those Iceberg or Delta tables directly into your own cloud buckets—like S3—so they sit alongside the rest of your lake data. Tableflow is currently available on both **AWS** and **Azure**. |
| 50 seconds | ![][image5] Show each catalog integration in the product Enable Databricks Show the results in Databricks  | Once data lands as tables, it needs to be discoverable. That’s where catalog integrations come in. By default, **Tableflow automatically registers your Iceberg tables in a Confluent‑managed Iceberg REST Catalog**, so they’re immediately queryable through an open, standards‑based interface. When you want to **extend an existing lakehouse to include real‑time streaming data**, Tableflow can also publish table metadata into other catalog systems, including: AWS Glue Apache Polaris / Snowflake Open Catalog Databricks Unity Catalog The result is simple: operational Kafka data shows up as open‑format tables in the catalogs and engines your analytics teams already use— like Snowflake, Databricks, AWS Athena/Redshift, Trino and more\!  |
| **Part 3 Performance, Data Quality, and Shift-Left (1.5 mins)** |  |  |
| **Timing** | **Visual** | **Narration** |
| 45 seconds  | Demo schema management, data contracts,   ** ![][image6]** | You might be thinking to yourself, “well this seems like just another connector”. Let’s talk about why it’s more than that. Tableflow is tightly integrated with **Confluent Schema Registry**, so it has first‑class schema management built in, supporting Avro, Protobuf, and JSON Schema, as well as automatically: Schematizing incoming Kafka records Applying schema evolution according to your compatibility rules ~~Enforcing data quality rules \- like removing PII from topic data~~ Handling type mappings and conversions into Parquet and the underlying table formats In practice, that means your tables inherit the same data contracts you’ve already defined at the stream layer. **You don’t have to rebuild those rules again in the lake\!** Second, unlike a traditional sink connector that pulls data off the brokers and ships it out to an external system, Tableflow is built directly on Kora’s tiered storage layer. It reads from the same durable object storage that backs your Kafka topics, which: Minimizes load on the brokers compared to connector-based approaches that continuously consume from live partitions Avoids extra egress traffic from streaming data out to a separate ingestion stack just to land it in tables Reduces redundant replication and copies of the same data, since you’re reusing the existing stored segments instead of duplicating them into yet another system Combined together, you get stronger schema guarantees *and* a more efficient architecture: ~~governed streams become analytics‑ready tables without overloading brokers, paying unnecessary egress, or maintaining a second, fully replicated copy of the same data just for your lake.~~  |
| 50 seconds | ![][image7] Demo creation of deduplication and denormalized data products in Flink Demo Tableflow enabled on ^^ data products | Additionally, Confluent Tableflow empowers you to remove complexity from your data streaming pipelines by taking care of automated maintenance and optimizations.  Streaming into object storage typically creates a lot of tiny files that kill query performance. Confluent Tableflow continuously compacts and cleans up those small files into larger, more efficient Parquet files—all without you writing any maintenance jobs\! ~~All of this pairs naturally with **Confluent Cloud for Apache Flink**, where you can shift your stream processing left by cleaning and enriching data in real time before it ever becomes a table. For example, with Flink you can: Deduplicate events Join multiple topics into a curated “data product” stream Perform windowed aggregations  Then Confluent Tableflow materializes these curated data products directly into Iceberg or Delta Lake. This helps avoid downstream transformations, saving compute and maintenance costs.~~ |
| 35 seconds | Demo tableflow handling **10k** inserts per second | For high‑change datasets, Tableflow also supports **high‑throughput upserts**.  You can define primary keys on your CDC streams, and Tableflow will continuously reconcile inserts, updates, and deletes into your target tables, at scale. Under the hood it manages compaction and merge efficiency, so even billions of rows can stay queryable with materialization lags measured in minutes, not hours—without you having to own a custom upsert pipeline.  |
| 30 seconds | Show enabling DLQ on Tableflow | And when something goes wrong, you don’t have to choose between “*fail hard*” and “*silently drop data*.”  Tableflow offers a **dead letter queue** (DLQ) for problematic records. You can configure whether bad records should suspend the flow, be skipped, or be routed into a dedicated Kafka topic with full error context. That means you keep your tables healthy and up to date, while still having a side channel where data engineers can inspect, fix, and replay skipped events. |
| **Part 4 Security and Governance (1.5 mins)** |  |  |
| **Timing** | **Visual** | **Narration** |
| ~~25 seconds~~ | ~~Demo BYOK~~ | ~~Confluent Tableflow is built for production‑grade security and governance. For data at rest, it supports Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) encryption, where you can: Use the same customer‑managed key that already encrypts your Kafka clusters in Confluent Managed Storage, or Use your own Key Management Service (KMS) keys for BYOS storage, so encryption and access policies stay aligned with your internal standards~~ |
| 30 seconds | Show schema evolution of a specific topic | In the real world, event schemas rarely stay static—fields get added, renamed, or deprecated. Tableflow supports  **schema evolution** by leaning on Schema Registry’s compatibility modes, so those changes are automatically reflected in your Iceberg or Delta tables, while staying compatible with existing consumers.  This empowers teams to iterate on schemas safely, without constantly rebuilding tables or risking silent data corruption. |
| 20 seconds | Demo Unity Catalog integration and access controls | ~~As shown earlier, Confluent Tableflow **catalog integrations** make data discovery extremely convenient. Additionally, they also plug into your existing governance stack, including role-based access control, lineage, and audit tooling, enabling you to manage streaming‑derived tables in the same way as the rest of your analytical estate~~. |
| **Part 5 Outro (0.25 mins)** |  |  |
| **Timing** | **Visual** | **Narration** |
| 20 seconds | ![][image8] | Streaming with Confluent Tableflow means you can leave behind custom, bespoke, and rigid data lake pipeline jobs and easily stream high-quality, governed, and enriched topics as open-format tables.  Stream with Confluent Cloud and Tableflow today\! OR To recap, Tableflow can…  Easily stream your Kafka topics as Iceberg and Delta open‑format tables **Improve your data quality and performance** by enforcing schemas, automating table maintenance, and seamlessly pairing with Flink for shift‑left processing Deliver **enterprise‑grade security and governance** for your data with BYOK encryption and deep catalog integrations |

# Appendix

The following are notes and information that were used to craft the talk track

### Key Features

**Materialization to Open Table Formats:** Expose Kafka topics or Flink tables as Iceberg or Delta Lake tables in just a few clicks.  
 [1](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/topics/tableflow/overview.html)  
**Flexible Storage Options:** Choose between Confluent Managed Storage or Bring Your Own Storage (BYOS) for materialized tables.  
 [1](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/topics/tableflow/overview.html)[4](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/topics/tableflow/get-started/overview.html)  
**Catalog Integrations:** Seamless integration with AWS Glue, Apache Polaris, Snowflake Open Catalog, and Iceberg REST Catalog for easy discovery and governance.  
 [1](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/topics/tableflow/overview.html)[5](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/topics/tableflow/concepts/overview.html)  
**Schema Management:** Leverages Confluent Schema Registry for automatic schematization and schema evolution, supporting Avro, Protobuf, and JSON Schema.  
 [1](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/topics/tableflow/overview.html)  
**Automated Table Maintenance:** Handles compaction and cleanup of small files for optimal read performance and storage efficiency.  
 [1](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/topics/tableflow/overview.html)  
**Change Data Capture (CDC):** Efficiently materializes CDC logs, preserving change event integrity for downstream analytics.  
 [1](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/topics/tableflow/overview.html)  
**Security:** Supports Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) encryption for compliance and enhanced data security.  
 [1](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/topics/tableflow/overview.html)[6](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Li8lylvPf9jVos74GESSN-GNMp6k-JmQQDn7oQeNh20)  
**Shift-Left Stream Processing:** Integrates with Confluent Cloud for Apache Flink, enabling real-time data enrichment and transformation before materialization.

### Use Cases

| Use Case | Tableflow Value Proposition |
| ----- | ----- |
| Real-Time Analytics | Instantly expose Kafka data as queryable tables for BI and reporting |
| Data Lake/Warehouse Ingestion | Automate ETL, reduce costs, and improve data quality |
| Customer 360/Personalization | Enrich and unify customer data upstream for analytics and AI |
| CDC Materialization | Replicate and transform change logs for analytics and compliance |
| AI/ML Enablement | Provide fresh, trustworthy data for GenAI and ML workloads |
| Inventory/Fraud/Operational | Power real-time dashboards and anomaly detection |
| Data Governance & Compliance | Enforce schema, mask PII, and support regulatory deletes |

### 

### **1\. Real-Time Analytics and Reporting**

**Scenario:** Organizations need to make operational data from Kafka instantly available for analytics, dashboards, and business intelligence—without building and maintaining complex ETL pipelines.

**How Tableflow Helps:** Tableflow materializes Kafka topics as Apache Iceberg or Delta Lake tables, making fresh, trustworthy data available to any analytics engine (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Trino, Athena) in near real-time. This eliminates the need for repetitive, error-prone batch jobs and reduces time-to-insight for business users.

[1](https://www.confluent.io/blog/introducing-tableflow/)[2](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Fhv7ZNoow1Cm0gC3VlJZvJ4lnRApY8N57Hbwlhp0Rd4)  
---

### **2\. Data Lake and Data Warehouse Ingestion (“Shift Left”)**

**Scenario:** Data teams spend significant time and money cleaning, transforming, and loading data from Kafka into data lakes or warehouses, often duplicating effort and driving up compute costs.

**How Tableflow Helps:** By automating the conversion of Kafka topics into open table formats, Tableflow enables organizations to “shift left” their data processing—performing cleansing, enrichment, and governance upstream in the streaming layer. This reduces downstream data quality issues, cuts warehouse compute costs by up to 30%, and improves data engineer productivity.

[2](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Fhv7ZNoow1Cm0gC3VlJZvJ4lnRApY8N57Hbwlhp0Rd4)[3](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/11e6L7gav0-h9MqBzYLrFeKCZzLmB-gCUTZ_W-GLV9Jw)

**Example:** A global food retailer replaced a complex, multi-vendor pipeline (Oracle → Goldengate → Azure Event Hubs → Ab Initio → Snowflake) with Confluent Cloud and Tableflow, saving over $3M by streamlining real-time stock data ingestion and reporting.

[2](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Fhv7ZNoow1Cm0gC3VlJZvJ4lnRApY8N57Hbwlhp0Rd4)  
---

### **3\. Customer 360 and Personalization**

**Scenario:** Companies want to build a unified, real-time view of customer activity for personalized marketing, support, and analytics, but struggle with expensive joins and data silos in their warehouse.

**How Tableflow Helps:** Tableflow, combined with Flink stream processing, allows organizations to tag and enrich customer data upstream, so that analytics-ready tables in the data lake or warehouse already contain the necessary context (e.g., customer IDs, behavioral tags). This enables faster, more accurate Customer 360 use cases and reduces the need for costly post-processing in the warehouse.

[2](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Fhv7ZNoow1Cm0gC3VlJZvJ4lnRApY8N57Hbwlhp0Rd4)

**Example:** A connected health products company automated customer ID tagging across 10 billion daily transactions, landing enriched data directly in Snowflake and saving $6M annually by eliminating duplicate datasets and expensive warehouse joins.

[2](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Fhv7ZNoow1Cm0gC3VlJZvJ4lnRApY8N57Hbwlhp0Rd4)  
---

### **4\. Change Data Capture (CDC) Materialization**

**Scenario:** Enterprises need to replicate source database tables (e.g., via Debezium CDC) into analytics-ready tables for reporting, compliance, or data science, but require upserts and data sanitization.

**How Tableflow Helps:** Tableflow can materialize CDC logs from Kafka topics into Iceberg or Delta Lake tables, preserving change event integrity and supporting downstream analytics. With Flink integration, organizations can also sanitize or flatten data in-stream before materialization, supporting compliance requirements like GDPR.

[4](https://confluentinc.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/KORAGE/pages/3638461472)

**Example:** Indeed is evaluating Tableflow to simplify their “Logrepo” use case, where hundreds of Kafka applications ingest data with multiple schemas. They need to sanitize PII, support GDPR deletes, and materialize CDC records for analytics—all of which Tableflow aims to address.

[4](https://confluentinc.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/KORAGE/pages/3638461472)  
---

### **5\. AI/ML and GenAI Enablement**

**Scenario:** Data science and AI teams need access to fresh, high-quality data for training, inference, and real-time recommendations, but often face stale or inconsistent data in the lakehouse.

**How Tableflow Helps:** By making streaming data instantly available as open tables, Tableflow supports AI/ML workloads that require both real-time and historical data. This is especially valuable for building Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and agentic AI use cases, where trustworthy, up-to-date data is critical.

[1](https://www.confluent.io/blog/introducing-tableflow/)[5](https://www.confluent.io/blog/building-ai-agents-and-copilots/)  
---

### **6\. Inventory Management, Fraud Detection, and Operational Analytics**

**Scenario:** Retailers, manufacturers, and financial institutions need real-time insights for inventory optimization, fraud detection, and operational decision-making.

**How Tableflow Helps:** Tableflow enables these organizations to continuously feed cleansed, aggregated, and normalized data into analytical platforms, powering dashboards, anomaly detection, and predictive analytics with minimal latency and maximum data quality.

[2](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Fhv7ZNoow1Cm0gC3VlJZvJ4lnRApY8N57Hbwlhp0Rd4)  
---

### **7\. Data Governance and Compliance**

**Scenario:** Organizations face regulatory requirements (e.g., GDPR) to delete or mask sensitive data, and need to ensure data lineage and quality from source to analytics.

**How Tableflow Helps:** Tableflow leverages Confluent Schema Registry for schema enforcement and supports integration with Flink for in-stream data sanitization and masking. This helps organizations maintain compliance and data trustworthiness throughout the data lifecycle.

[4](https://confluentinc.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/KORAGE/pages/3638461472)
